concerns.text: Task T023 implements PCA reduction to 50 components, but FR-008 in spec.md explicitly requires reduction to 200 components. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' dependency: the downstream modeling tasks (T024-T028) consume a feature set defined by the spec (200 dims), but the producer task (T023) emits a different dimensionality (50 dims), breaking the semantic contract required for the baseline comparison.

Task T022 (Compute 200x200 connectivity matrix) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, the plan.md 'Computational Feasibility Strategy' explicitly states this requires 'chunked processing' with 'disk spillover' to avoid RAM overflow. If T022 runs in parallel across subjects without explicit locking or unique output paths per subject, it risks race conditions on the shared disk spillover files, violating the [P] safety guarantee.

Task T017 (Filter subjects < 100 time points) is placed in Phase 3 (US1 Implementation). However, T005 (Data Loader) and T009 (Preprocessing skeleton) are in Phase 2. The exclusion logic (FR-007) is a prerequisite for generating the valid input for T013 (Motion Scrubbing) and T015 (Entropy). While T017 is technically part of the US1 flow, the exclusion check should logically precede or be tightly coupled with the initial data loading (T005) to prevent processing invalid subjects in T013/T015. The current order risks running T013 on subjects that will be immediately discarded by T017.
